question: Will CPI rise more than 0.4% in April 2026?
reasoning: Based on current economic conditions, April CPI will likely exceed 0.4%. The Iran war began February 28, causing massive energy price spikes - gasoline rose from ~$3.20 to over $4.00 per gallon and oil jumped from ~$80 to $110+ per barrel. March CPI is forecast at 0.9-1.0% monthly due to energy shocks. While April may see some moderation as ceasefire talks emerge, elevated oil prices ($95-110 range) and supply chain disruptions will maintain significant inflationary pressure through Q2 2026.
key_factors: Iran war energy shock driving oil to $110+ per barrel
Gasoline prices increased ~30% from $3.20 to over $4.00 per gallon
March CPI forecasted at 0.9-1.0% monthly by economists
Strait of Hormuz disruptions affecting 20% of global oil supply
Food and transportation costs rising due to diesel price surge
risks: Potential ceasefire agreement could rapidly reduce oil prices
Strategic Petroleum Reserve releases may dampen price increases
Base effects from prior year comparisons could moderate readings
Federal Reserve intervention or policy changes
Alternative energy supply routes becoming operational
